text.title: API Error Handling Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-error-handling work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every endpoint returns a different error shape; partner SDK has a per-endpoint switch statement.
antipattern.root-cause: No standardised envelope; each developer rolled their own.
antipattern.fix: Adopt RFC 7807; centralise via middleware. See rule rfc-7807-envelope.

antipattern.symptom: Customer complaint says "request failed at 14:32"; support has no traceId; engineers grep logs by IP.
antipattern.root-cause: Tracer present but errors do not surface span id.
antipattern.fix: Middleware that copies active span trace_id into error body. See rule traceid-required.

antipattern.symptom: Invalid input returns 500; oncall paged at 3am for what is the user's typo.
antipattern.root-cause: Exception handler catches all and emits 500.
antipattern.fix: Per-exception mapping: ValidationError → 400; AuthError → 401; etc. See rule 4xx-vs-5xx-split.

antipattern.symptom: Production 500 returns full Python traceback including DB hostname + query.
antipattern.fix: Strip traceback before serialisation; log full trace server-side with traceId. See rule no-pii-in-error.
